claim: Spatial intelligence (reasoning, understanding, and interaction in 3D/4D space) is a distinct form of intelligence from linguistic intelligence and represents the next frontier after LLMs
actionable: Recognize that building AGI may require spatial intelligence capabilities alongside language; consider how your models can understand 3D geometry, physics, and embodied interaction
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Language is a lossy, low-bandwidth channel for describing the rich 3D world; you can speak ~215,000 tokens per day but live in far higher-bandwidth sensory reality
actionable: When designing multimodal models, don't assume language captures all relevant information; incorporate vision, spatial data, and interactive simulation alongside text
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: World models (generative 3D systems) are a promising way to 'soak up' modern GPU compute better than language alone, given ~1 millionfold increase in compute since AlexNet
actionable: If you have access to large-scale compute, consider world models or spatial generative tasks as more compute-efficient targets than pure language scaling
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Marble (World Labs' model) generates editable 3D worlds from text/images as Gaussian splats, enabling real-time rendering on phones/VR, precise camera control, and interactive scene editing
actionable: Explore Marble for rapid 3D scene generation in gaming, VFX, film, interior design, and robotics simulation use cases; use advanced edit mode to refine individual objects
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Gaussian splats (tiny semi-transparent particles with 3D position/orientation) are an efficient atomic unit for generative 3D worlds that render in real-time on mobile
actionable: If building 3D generative models, consider Gaussian splatting as a renderer; it's compute-efficient and enables client-side real-time rendering
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Today's generative models (including Marble) learn pattern-fitting but do not truly model physics/causality; they generate plausible outputs but can fail on counterfactuals or new scenarios
actionable: Don't assume a visually plausible 3D world from a generative model actually obeys physics; use classical physics engines + learned properties for critical applications (architecture, robotics)
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Language models can predict orbital movements accurately from data but don't learn the causal law (F=MA, gravity) needed to generalize to new scenarios or draw force vectors
actionable: When you need causal understanding (not just prediction), don't rely solely on learned patterns; inject domain knowledge or traditional simulations into your model architecture
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Transformers are natively models of sets (via permutation-equivariance), not sequences; positional embeddings inject order, but QKV/FFN/attention are set operations
actionable: Understand that transformers are flexible enough for 3D/spatial data by treating spatial coordinates or object properties as set elements; you don't need fundamentally new architectures
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Humans learn causal physics by active hypothesis testing (trying things, observing failures, updating beliefs) more than passive observation—models need similar feedback loops
actionable: Build world models with active learning loops: let models make predictions, compare to real outcomes, falsify incorrect theories, and update beliefs
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Synthetic simulation data (from physics engines or procedural generation) is crucial for robotics training where real-world high-fidelity data is scarce
actionable: For embodied AI training, invest in procedural world generation and simulation to create diverse, controllable training scenarios; don't rely only on real-world capture
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Vision and spatial intelligence are underappreciated by humans because they feel effortless (mostly innate), whereas language is effortful (learned), making language seem 'harder' and more important
actionable: Don't discount vision in your AI systems design just because it seems 'easy'; nature spent 540M years optimizing perception vs. ~500k for language
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Emergent capabilities in large models (like discovering security bugs, inferring physics) may not generalize across modalities; pixel-to-physics and language-to-physics are different learning problems
actionable: Don't assume that scaling a visual world model will automatically transfer causal understanding from language or other domains; test generalization empirically
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: New hardware (beyond GPUs) and new primitives (beyond matrix multiplication) may be needed to scale world models efficiently; current GPU bottlenecks hint at this
actionable: If investing long-term in world models, explore alternative compute paradigms (neuromorphic, analog, specialized architectures) rather than assuming GPU scaling continues infinitely
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Using traditional physics engines to generate training data, then distilling into neural network weights, is a practical hybrid approach for building physically plausible models
actionable: Generate training data for world models by running classical physics simulations (like video games do); this injects physics knowledge without explicitly encoding it
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Academia's role in AI is shifting from training largest models (which industry wins) to exploring wacky ideas, new architectures, and blue-sky problems with limited compute
actionable: If in academia, stop trying to beat industry at scale; instead, focus on novel algorithms, new architectures, and fundamental insights that scale later
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs

claim: Open science (challenges, benchmarks, datasets) remains important for ecosystem health but must coexist with proprietary industry work; both have roles
actionable: Support and contribute to open datasets/benchmarks (e.g., BEHAVIOR for robotics) even as industry ships closed products; diversity in approaches drives progress
source_title: After LLMs: Spatial Intelligence and World Models — Fei-Fei Li & Justin Johnson, World Labs
